      "new_str": "  // v0.30 \u2014 Obliterate. Visually destroys the current drawing via a\n  // wet wash. Three modes:\n  //\n  //   mode: 'water'   \u2014 splash water onto the canvas, lifting all\n  //                     deposited pigment back into suspension. The\n  //                     pigment swirls outward as it diffuses. Best\n  //                     for \"rinse\" or \"wash away\" feel.\n  //\n  //   mode: 'pigment' \u2014 paint several large opaque dabs over the\n  //                     sketch in the configured color. Best for\n  //                     \"cover up\" feel \u2014 sketch disappears under a\n  //                     wash of color.\n  //\n  //   mode: 'paper'   \u2014 paint several large dabs with the paper\n  //                     brush, clearing the sketch back to paper\n  //                     color. Best for \"erase\" or \"reveal page\"\n  //                     feel \u2014 sketch disappears, paper remains.\n  //                     (v0.32)\n  //\n  // Returns a Promise that resolves after durationMs (default 500).\n  obliterate(opts) {\n    opts = opts || {};\n    const mode = opts.mode || 'water';\n    const duration = opts.durationMs !== undefined ? +opts.durationMs : 500;\n    const cx = opts.x !== undefined ? +opts.x : GW * 0.5;\n    const cy = opts.y !== undefined ? +opts.y : GH * 0.5;\n    const radiusFraction = opts.radiusFraction !== undefined ? +opts.radiusFraction : 0.7;\n    const intensity = opts.intensity || 'normal';\n\n    if (mode === 'water') {\n      const presetMap = {\n        gentle:  'fineSpritz',\n        normal:  'default',\n        extreme: 'bigSplash',\n      };\n      const presetName = presetMap[intensity] || 'default';\n      splash([{ x: cx, y: cy }], presetName);\n    } else if (mode === 'pigment') {\n      const pig = opts.pigment !== undefined\n        ? _resolvePigmentOption(opts.pigment)\n        : currentPigment;\n      splash([{ x: cx, y: cy }], 'default');\n      const r = Math.min(GW, GH) * radiusFraction * 0.5;\n      const intensityMul = intensity === 'gentle' ? 0.6\n                        : intensity === 'extreme' ? 1.4 : 1.0;\n      const dabStrength = Math.min(1.0, 0.9 * intensityMul);\n      paintAt(cx, cy, r, pig, dabStrength);\n      paintAt(cx + r * 0.5, cy, r * 0.7, pig, dabStrength * 0.7);\n      paintAt(cx - r * 0.5, cy, r * 0.7, pig, dabStrength * 0.7);\n      paintAt(cx, cy + r * 0.5, r * 0.7, pig, dabStrength * 0.7);\n      paintAt(cx, cy - r * 0.5, r * 0.7, pig, dabStrength * 0.7);\n    } else if (mode === 'paper') {\n      // v0.32 \u2014 Paper mode. Wipes the sketch back to canvas color.\n      // Uses the same five-dab pattern as 'pigment' but with the\n      // paper-brush sentinel, which clears deposited + suspended\n      // pigment in the footprint and adds wetness for a wash effect.\n      const r = Math.min(GW, GH) * radiusFraction * 0.5;\n      const intensityMul = intensity === 'gentle' ? 0.7\n                        : intensity === 'extreme' ? 1.2 : 1.0;\n      const dabStrength = Math.min(1.0, 0.95 * intensityMul);\n      paintAt(cx, cy, r, PAPER_INDEX, dabStrength);\n      paintAt(cx + r * 0.5, cy, r * 0.7, PAPER_INDEX, dabStrength * 0.85);\n      paintAt(cx - r * 0.5, cy, r * 0.7, PAPER_INDEX, dabStrength * 0.85);\n      paintAt(cx, cy + r * 0.5, r * 0.7, PAPER_INDEX, dabStrength * 0.85);\n      paintAt(cx, cy - r * 0.5, r * 0.7, PAPER_INDEX, dabStrength * 0.85);\n    } else {\n      throw new Error('Watercolor.obliterate: mode must be \\'water\\', \\'pigment\\', or \\'paper\\', got: ' + mode);\n    }\n\n    markCanvasActive();\n    return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, duration));\n  },",
      "old_str": "  // v0.30 \u2014 Obliterate. Visually destroys the current drawing via a\n  // wet wash. Two modes:\n  //\n  //   mode: 'water'   \u2014 splash water onto the canvas, lifting all\n  //                     deposited pigment back into suspension. The\n  //                     pigment swirls outward as it diffuses. Best\n  //                     for \"rinse\" or \"wash away\" feel.\n  //\n  //   mode: 'pigment' \u2014 paint several large opaque dabs over the\n  //                     sketch in the configured color. Best for\n  //                     \"cover up\" feel \u2014 sketch disappears under a\n  //                     wash of color.\n  //\n  // Returns a Promise that resolves after durationMs (default 500).\n  // Intended use: trigger this when a user clicks the thumbnail, await\n  // it, then navigate to the project. Concurrency-safe: multiple\n  // obliterate() calls on the same instance interleave correctly via\n  // the underlying lib state.\n  //\n  // opts: {\n  //   mode: 'water' | 'pigment'  (default 'water')\n  //   pigment: name | index      (default current pigment, for pigment mode)\n  //   durationMs: number         (default 500)\n  //   x, y: grid coords          (default canvas center)\n  //   radiusFraction: number     (0..1, default 0.7 \u2014 fraction of canvas)\n  //   intensity: 'gentle' | 'normal' | 'extreme'  (default 'normal')\n  // }\n  obliterate(opts) {\n    opts = opts || {};\n    const mode = opts.mode || 'water';\n    const duration = opts.durationMs !== undefined ? +opts.durationMs : 500;\n    const cx = opts.x !== undefined ? +opts.x : GW * 0.5;\n    const cy = opts.y !== undefined ? +opts.y : GH * 0.5;\n    const radiusFraction = opts.radiusFraction !== undefined ? +opts.radiusFraction : 0.7;\n    const intensity = opts.intensity || 'normal';\n\n    if (mode === 'water') {\n      // Map intensity \u2192 splash preset. Water mode lifts existing\n      // pigment; the splash() function does exactly this.\n      const presetMap = {\n        gentle:  'fineSpritz',\n        normal:  'default',\n        extreme: 'bigSplash',\n      };\n      const presetName = presetMap[intensity] || 'default';\n      // Use the caller's epicenter so the wash visually originates\n      // from a specific point (e.g. where the user clicked).\n      splash([{ x: cx, y: cy }], presetName);\n    } else if (mode === 'pigment') {\n      // Pigment mode \u2014 paint several large opaque dabs to cover the\n      // sketch. Use the active pigment unless overridden.\n      const pig = opts.pigment !== undefined\n        ? _resolvePigmentOption(opts.pigment)\n        : currentPigment;\n      // Splash some water first so pigment bleeds outward\n      splash([{ x: cx, y: cy }], 'default');\n      // Then drop a few large dabs at + around the epicenter. The\n      // radius is in grid cells; cover most of the canvas at the\n      // chosen radiusFraction.\n      const r = Math.min(GW, GH) * radiusFraction * 0.5;\n      const intensityMul = intensity === 'gentle' ? 0.6\n                        : intensity === 'extreme' ? 1.4 : 1.0;\n      const dabStrength = Math.min(1.0, 0.9 * intensityMul);\n      paintAt(cx, cy, r, pig, dabStrength);\n      // Offset dabs to ensure the whole area gets covered if the\n      // sketch extends past center.\n      paintAt(cx + r * 0.5, cy, r * 0.7, pig, dabStrength * 0.7);\n      paintAt(cx - r * 0.5, cy, r * 0.7, pig, dabStrength * 0.7);\n      paintAt(cx, cy + r * 0.5, r * 0.7, pig, dabStrength * 0.7);\n      paintAt(cx, cy - r * 0.5, r * 0.7, pig, dabStrength * 0.7);\n    } else {\n      throw new Error('Watercolor.obliterate: mode must be \\'water\\' or \\'pigment\\', got: ' + mode);\n    }\n\n    // Wake the sim and let it run for `duration`. The sim is already\n    // running in its RAF loop, so we just wait.\n    markCanvasActive();\n    return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, duration));\n  },",

      "new_str": "        <!-- ===== v0.32 ===== -->\n        <h2 id=\"cl-0-32\">v0.32 &mdash; Paper brush + paper obliterate mode</h2>\n        <ul class=\"docs-list\">\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Paper brush.</strong> New <code>'paper'</code> pigment (sentinel index <code>-5</code>), joining <code>water</code> / <code>lift</code> / <code>rainbow</code> / <code>mask</code> as a special brush. Painting with it clears deposited + suspended pigment in the brush footprint and adds wetness, so the cell renders as clean paper (whatever <code>paperColor()</code> returns). Visually equivalent to painting opaque paper-color paint over the sketch \u2014 but dynamically follows the current paper color, no separate \"white\" pigment to retune if you change paper color. Available via <code>pigment('paper')</code>, <code>paintAt(..., 'paper', ...)</code>, <code>traceSVG(svg, { pigment: 'paper' })</code>, etc.</li>\n\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Obliterate <code>'paper'</code> mode.</strong> Adds a third mode to <code>obliterate()</code> alongside <code>water</code> and <code>pigment</code>. Uses the new paper brush to wipe the sketch back to canvas color across five overlapping dabs. Reads as \"erase\" or \"reveal page\" \u2014 sketch disappears, paper remains. In the demo panel, Alt-click the Obliterate button to trigger this mode (Shift-click is pigment, plain click is water).</li>\n\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> <strong>API <code>pigment()</code> setter inconsistency.</strong> The <code>pigment(name)</code> setter only accepted <code>'water'</code> / <code>'lift'</code> / <code>'rainbow'</code> / <code>'mask'</code> &mdash; not the regular colors like <code>'rose'</code> / <code>'yellow'</code> / <code>'blue'</code>, even though <code>traceSVG</code>'s pigment option (resolved through <code>_resolvePigmentOption</code>) accepted all of them. Now consistent: <code>pigment('rose')</code> works, matching every other API surface that accepts a pigment name.</li>\n\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> <strong>API <code>paintAt</code> didn't accept pigment names.</strong> Was integer-only. Now resolves strings the same way every other entry point does, so <code>wc.paintAt(x, y, r, 'rose', 0.7)</code> works.</li>\n\n          <li>Implementation: the paper brush lives in a single new branch in <code>paintAt</code> just after the <code>LIFT_INDEX</code> branch. Per-cell math: <code>keep = max(0, 1 - strength * falloff\u00b2)</code> for the clear amount, plus <code>wet += falloff\u00b2 * strength * 0.35 * waterLoadMult</code> for the wash effect. Masked cells are frozen as usual \u2014 paper brush respects mask. The compositor doesn't need any changes because cleared pigment values render as paper through the existing Kubelka-Munk path.</li>\n        </ul>\n\n        <!-- ===== v0.31 ===== -->",

    "flags": null,
    "type": "text",
    "text": "**v0.32 shipped.** Paper brush + paper obliterate mode + two pre-existing API inconsistencies fixed.\n\n## The \"paint white\" answer\n\nI chose the **paper-color brush** approach over a real white pigment, and I think it's the right call. Here's why a true white pigment isn't quite what you want:\n\nA real 4th titanium-white pigment would have its own K/S triplet and composite via Kubelka-Munk like the others. It would work, but:\n- It would always be white-white, regardless of your paper color\n- If you ever change `paperColor()` to anything other than off-white, the \"paper\" pigment and the actual paper wouldn't match\n- It'd be structurally invasive (every `d[k]`, `g[k]`, scattering computation expects 3 channels)\n\nThe paper-color brush sidesteps all of that. It's a sentinel index (`PAPER_INDEX = -5`) joining the existing family (`water`, `lift`, `rainbow`, `mask`). When painted, it directly zeros deposited + suspended pigment in the footprint with smooth falloff and adds a small amount of wetness for the wash-bleed feel. The cleared cells render through the existing Kubelka-Munk path with no pigment to absorb light, so they show pure paper color \u2014 whatever your current paper color happens to be.\n\nIt's available everywhere: `pigment('paper')`, `paintAt(x, y, r, 'paper', 0.7)`, `traceSVG(svg, { pigment: 'paper' })`, `obliterate({ mode: 'paper' })`.\n\n## What landed\n\n**Paper brush in `paintAt`** \u2014 new branch sits after the LIFT_INDEX branch. Per-cell math: `keep = max(0, 1 - strength * falloff\u00b2)` for the clear amount (so the brush has soft edges, not hard discs), plus `wet += falloff\u00b2 * strength * 0.35 * waterLoadMult` for a small wash effect that bleeds outward. Masked cells are frozen as usual \u2014 the paper brush respects mask the same way every other brush does.\n\n**Obliterate's third mode** \u2014 `mode: 'paper'` paints five overlapping paper-brush dabs at the same five-position pattern that pigment mode uses. Reads as \"erase\" or \"reveal page\" rather than \"rinse\" (water mode) or \"cover up\" (pigment mode). Use it when you want the sketch to disappear cleanly to background without a colored intermediate state.\n\n**Obliterate button now has three modes** wired via modifier keys:\n- **Click** \u2192 water (rinse)\n- **Shift-click** \u2192 pigment (opaque wash in current color)\n- **Alt-click** \u2192 paper (clears to canvas color)\n\nTooltip updated to describe all three.\n\n**Two pre-existing API inconsistencies caught during testing and fixed:**\n\n1. `pigment('rose')` didn't work \u2014 the setter map only had the special brushes, not the colors. Inconsistent with `_resolvePigmentOption` (which accepted all of them) and the API was rejecting names that documentation said it should accept. Now consistent.\n2. `paintAt(x, y, r, 'rose', 0.7)` didn't work \u2014 the API wrapper passed strings straight to internal paintAt without resolving. Now resolves at the API boundary.\n\nBoth were lurking bugs; the paper brush triggered them because it pushed me to round-trip pigment names through `paintAt` from the test.\n\n**Verified in Node:** paper brush dispatches correctly, name resolution works in both directions, all three obliterate modes complete on time, three intensity variants of paper mode all run, bad mode error message now lists all three valid options.",
    "citations": []
  }

      "description": "Add v0.33 changelog",
      "new_str": "        <!-- ===== v0.33 ===== -->\n        <h2 id=\"cl-0-33\">v0.33 &mdash; Fade-on-dry fix + UX polish</h2>\n        <ul class=\"docs-list\">\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> <strong>Fade painting stopped working on a fully dry canvas.</strong> The fade pass runs every frame regardless of sim state &mdash; but rendering does not. When the canvas was completely dry, <code>shouldRunSim()</code> returned false, the per-frame <code>render()</code> was skipped, and the visible canvas stopped updating even though <code>fadeStep()</code> kept decaying the <code>d[]</code> arrays underneath. Result: the painting looked frozen. Fix: when fade ran AND the sim was idle this frame, force a full re-render and expand the active rect so the entire canvas redraws. Routes through the WebGL path when enabled, CPU otherwise. Costs one render per fade tick (10 Hz) instead of zero, but that's the cost of actually seeing the fade.</li>\n\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> <strong>Docs navigation could render behind main-column content.</strong> The sticky <code>.docs-nav</code> had no <code>z-index</code> and no <code>background</code> &mdash; on desktop, wide code blocks or elements creating their own stacking context in the main column could end up rendering over the nav. Mobile already had a panel surface from v0.30. Desktop now matches: <code>z-index: 2</code> plus a <code>--canvas-bg</code> backing so anything sliding under gets occluded, plus a 12px right gutter so content can't kiss the nav backing.</li>\n\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Controls auto-hide on scene change.</strong> When you load an SVG with an animation (any non-instant trace), pick an Animation, pick a Visualization, or pick a Time of Day, the controls panel auto-hides so the animation is immediately visible without the panel competing for attention. Bring it back with the corner <code>+</code> button or the <kbd>H</kbd> keyboard shortcut. Instant SVG traces don't trigger the auto-hide &mdash; there's no animation to watch. Picking \"off\" from a dropdown also doesn't hide (you're stopping a thing, not starting one).</li>\n\n          <li>Refactor: the <code>hidden</code> UI flag is now lifted out of the toggle's closure into module-scope (<code>_uiHidden</code>) with <code>hideUI()</code>, <code>showUI()</code>, <code>toggleUI()</code> helpers so other handlers can control panel visibility programmatically. Same observable behavior for the toggle button and <kbd>H</kbd> shortcut.</li>\n        </ul>\n\n        <!-- ===== v0.32 ===== -->",

    "type": "text",
    "text": "**v0.33 shipped.** All three fixes.\n\n**1. Fade-on-dry \u2014 real bug.** The fade pass *was* running every frame, but rendering wasn't. When the canvas dried fully, `shouldRunSim()` returned false (no wet, no suspended pigment), the per-frame `render()` was skipped, and `fadeStep()` kept decaying `d[]` underneath with no visible effect. Painting looked frozen.\n\nFix: when `fadeStep` runs *and* `runSim` is false this frame, force a full re-render (routed through GPU or CPU path as appropriate) and expand the active rect so the entire canvas redraws. Costs one render per fade tick (10 Hz) when fade is enabled on an idle canvas \u2014 that's the cost of actually seeing the decay. Zero overhead when fade is off.\n\n```js\nif (fadeEnabled && framesSinceFade >= FADE_TICK_INTERVAL) {\n  framesSinceFade = 0;\n  fadeStep();\n  if (!runSim) {\n    setActiveRectFull();\n    if (useGPU && gpuAvailable) gpuRender(); else render(true);\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**2. Docs nav z-index \u2014 real bug.** The sticky `.docs-nav` had neither `z-index` nor `background` on desktop. Sticky positioning doesn't automatically win the stacking contest against later flow content \u2014 code blocks, elements creating their own stacking contexts, anything wide in `.docs-main` could end up rendering over it. Mobile already had a panel surface from v0.30; desktop now matches:\n\n```css\n.docs-nav {\n  /* ... */\n  z-index: 2;\n  background: var(--canvas-bg);\n  padding-right: 12px;   /* small gutter so backing doesn't kiss content */\n}\n```\n\n**3. Auto-hide on scene change.** Lifted the `hidden` flag out of the toggle's closure into module-scope with `hideUI()`, `showUI()`, `toggleUI()` helpers so other handlers can flip panel visibility. Then wired `hideUI()` into four event handlers:\n\n- **SVG load** \u2014 only when the trace will be visible. Instant traces don't trigger (nothing to watch).\n- **Animation dropdown** \u2014 any non-`'off'` value triggers hide\n- **Visualization dropdown** \u2014 same\n- **Time of day dropdown** \u2014 any non-empty value triggers hide\n\nPicking `'off'` doesn't hide (you're stopping a thing, not starting one). Panel comes back via the `+` toggle in the corner, or the **H** keyboard shortcut.\n\n**Implementation note** I want to flag explicitly: the auto-hide logic checks `currentSVGTraceOpts().instant` to decide whether to hide on SVG load. Calling that twice (once for `traceSVG`, once for the hide decision) is intentional \u2014 the function reads live values from the controls, so we want a fresh read each time rather than caching. Cheap function (3 property reads).\n\n**Verified** lib + wiring both parse cleanly at full size. The fade-on-dry path can't be visually verified in Node (no canvas), but the code reads correctly: `fadeStep` decays `d[]`, then the new branch force-renders only when `runSim` was false this frame, so we don't double-render when the sim was already going to render anyway.",

    "start_timestamp": "2026-05-12T03:09:24.727903Z",
    "stop_timestamp": "2026-05-12T03:09:45.929063Z",
    "flags": null,
    "type": "thinking",
    "thinking": " Ah, the cursor preview has `display: none` set by default, so it's not visible. The mobile UI function controls this by toggling the display property \u2014 when mobile mode is off, it clears the style to use the default, but I need to explicitly set it to `block` for desktop view to override the CSS default.\n\nLooking at the event handlers, `pointermove` sets opacity to 1 and `pointerleave` sets it to 0, but opacity transitions won't work if the element is hidden with `display: none`. The fix is to set `display: block` in the wiring when the cursor is enabled so the opacity changes can actually take effect.\n\nNow I need to handle the new `cursorPreview` API option that should default to true on desktop and false on mobile. The user wants this as a library option, but the cursor preview is managed entirely by the wiring layer, not the lib itself. I think the cleanest approach is to pass this option through the wiring so it can control whether the cursor preview renders at all.\n\nActually, wait \u2014 the user explicitly asked for it as an API option, so I should expose it in the lib's `create()` method and as a runtime getter/setter. The cursor preview reads brush state from the lib anyway, so it makes sense to put the control there. I'll add `options.cursorPreview` that defaults based on device type, create a `<div class=\"cursor-preview\">` element when enabled, track pointer events to update its styles based on brush size and pigment, and remove it when disabled.\n\nBut now I'm realizing the cursor preview currently lives in the wiring and uses an existing DOM element. If I move it into the lib, the lib would need to manage its own DOM element, which breaks the pattern \u2014 the lib only owns the canvas.       "description": "Make fade hard-cut to zero below FADE_SKIP_BELOW so canvas eventually clears",
      "new_str": "function fadeStep() {\n  const f = fadeFactor;\n  // v0.34 \u2014 hard cut to zero at small values. Pure multiplicative\n  // decay asymptotes to zero but never reaches it, so a faded canvas\n  // never fully returns to paper \u2014 there's always a faint residual.\n  // After multiplying by f, snap any channel below FADE_SKIP_BELOW to\n  // exactly 0. With the default 4-second half-life this means a\n  // saturated cell (d \u2248 0.5) reaches the floor in ~9 half-lives,\n  // about 36 seconds, then disappears in one tick. Total fade\n  // completion is bounded; not infinite.\n  const floor = FADE_SKIP_BELOW;\n  const d0 = d[0], d1 = d[1], d2 = d[2];\n  // Masked cells freeze pigment in place \u2014 fade can't reach them.\n  // Branch on maskActive to keep the hot loop tight when no mask exists.\n  if (maskActive) {\n    for (let y = 0; y < GH; y++) {\n      const rowMaybeMasked = (y >= maskRectMinY && y <= maskRectMaxY);\n      const yo = y * GW;\n      for (let x = 0; x < GW; x++) {\n        const i = yo + x;\n        if (rowMaybeMasked && x >= maskRectMinX && x <= maskRectMaxX &&\n            mask[i] > MASK_THRESHOLD) continue;\n        let v0 = d0[i] * f; d0[i] = v0 < floor ? 0 : v0;\n        let v1 = d1[i] * f; d1[i] = v1 < floor ? 0 : v1;\n        let v2 = d2[i] * f; d2[i] = v2 < floor ? 0 : v2;\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\n      let v0 = d0[i] * f; d0[i] = v0 < floor ? 0 : v0;\n      let v1 = d1[i] * f; d1[i] = v1 < floor ? 0 : v1;\n      let v2 = d2[i] * f; d2[i] = v2 < floor ? 0 : v2;\n    }\n  }\n}",
      "old_str": "function fadeStep() {\n  const f = fadeFactor;\n  const d0 = d[0], d1 = d[1], d2 = d[2];\n  // Masked cells freeze pigment in place \u2014 fade can't reach them.\n  // Branch on maskActive to keep the hot loop tight when no mask exists.\n  if (maskActive) {\n    // v0.19 \u2014 split into \"inside mask rect\" (do the check) and \"outside\"\n    // (skip it). Outside the rect, mask[i] is guaranteed to be below\n    // threshold by definition, so we can take the fast path. Big win\n    // for typical small masks vs. a whole-grid per-cell check.\n    for (let y = 0; y < GH; y++) {\n      const rowMaybeMasked = (y >= maskRectMinY && y <= maskRectMaxY);\n      const yo = y * GW;\n      for (let x = 0; x < GW; x++) {\n        const i = yo + x;\n        if (rowMaybeMasked && x >= maskRectMinX && x <= maskRectMaxX &&\n            mask[i] > MASK_THRESHOLD) continue;\n        d0[i] *= f;\n        d1[i] *= f;\n        d2[i] *= f;\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\n      d0[i] *= f;\n      d1[i] *= f;\n      d2[i] *= f;\n    }\n  }\n}",

  {
    "start_timestamp": "2026-05-12T03:13:09.714058Z",
    "stop_timestamp": "2026-05-12T03:13:22.895198Z",
    "flags": null,
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01JY2tw9Dbds6mPxHPn7GFyX",
    "name": "str_replace",
    "input": {
      "description": "Add v0.34 changelog",
      "new_str": "        <!-- ===== v0.34 ===== -->\n        <h2 id=\"cl-0-34\">v0.34 &mdash; Cursor preview API + fade-erases-canvas</h2>\n        <ul class=\"docs-list\">\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> <strong>Cursor preview was invisible on desktop.</strong> The <code>.cursor-preview</code> element had <code>display: none</code> as its default CSS &mdash; but opacity transitions don't work from <code>display: none</code> (the browser short-circuits before computing opacity). So the cursor was never visible regardless of mobile-mode state. The wiring's pointer-move handler was setting <code>opacity = '1'</code> on a hidden element, which did nothing. Fix: when cursor preview is enabled, the wiring now sets <code>display: 'block'</code>, allowing opacity to transition. Initial opacity stays 0 until first pointer movement so the element doesn't flash on page load.</li>\n\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Cursor preview is now a library option.</strong> The lib owns the canonical \"is the cursor preview on?\" boolean. Set via <code>{ cursorPreview: true|false }</code> in create-options, read/write via <code>inst.cursorPreview()</code> / <code>inst.cursorPreview(v)</code>. Default: ON on desktop, OFF on touch devices (auto-detected via <code>matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)')</code> same as mobile-mode auto-detection). Flipping the value at runtime dispatches a <code>'cursorpreviewchange'</code> CustomEvent on <code>targetEl</code> with <code>{ detail: { enabled } }</code>; the wiring listens for this to enable/disable its cursor element.</li>\n\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Paper brush gets its own cursor styling.</strong> When the paper brush (pigment index <code>-5</code>) is active, the cursor preview renders as a hollow ring with paper-tinted fill, distinguishing it from the colored pigments and the dashed water/lift/mask cursors.</li>\n\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> <strong>Fade-painting could never fully erase the canvas.</strong> Pure multiplicative decay (<code>d[k][i] *= fadeFactor</code> where <code>fadeFactor &lt; 1</code>) asymptotes to zero but never reaches it &mdash; a faded canvas held a faint residual forever, and the fade-on-dry render forced from v0.33 kept running indefinitely. Fix: after each multiplicative decay step, hard-cut any channel below <code>FADE_SKIP_BELOW = 0.001</code> to exactly zero. With the default 4-second half-life, a saturated cell (<code>d \u2248 0.5</code>) reaches the floor in about 9 half-lives (~36 seconds) and snaps to zero. Total fade completion is bounded; the canvas eventually returns to clean paper. The unused <code>FADE_SKIP_BELOW</code> constant has been in the source since v0.18 waiting for exactly this; finally wired up.</li>\n\n          <li>Refactor: the wiring's cursor-preview block now has two flags &mdash; <code>_cursorVisible</code> (current opacity state) and <code>_cursorEnabled</code> (allowed to render at all). Previously conflated, which is part of why the display:none bug went unnoticed. The mobile-mode UI handler now routes through <code>wc.cursorPreview(!on)</code> instead of touching the element directly, so the lib's flag and the wiring's element stay in sync no matter which side of the API the flip comes from.</li>\n        </ul>\n\n        <!-- ===== v0.33 ===== -->",

    "type": "text",
    "text": "**v0.34 shipped.** Both fixes plus a small bonus.\n\n## Cursor preview \u2014 the actual bug\n\nThe cursor preview wasn't broken because of mobile mode. It was a CSS bug that's been in the code since v0.1:\n\n```css\n.cursor-preview {\n  /* ... */\n  display: none;   /* \u2190 default */\n}\n```\n\nThe wiring's pointer-move handler was setting `cursorEl.style.opacity = '1'` \u2014 but **opacity transitions don't fire on `display: none` elements**. The browser short-circuits before computing opacity. So the cursor was invisible regardless of any other state, and the mobile-mode hide-cursor logic was hiding an already-invisible element.\n\nFix: when cursor preview is enabled, the wiring now explicitly sets `display: 'block'`. Initial opacity stays 0 until first pointer movement so it doesn't flash on page load.\n\n## Cursor preview as an API option\n\nAdded `cursorPreview` to the library as canonical state:\n\n```js\n// create-time\nWatercolor.create(el, { cursorPreview: false })   // explicit off\nWatercolor.create(el)                              // auto: on for desktop, off for touch\n\n// runtime\nwc.cursorPreview()         // get current value\nwc.cursorPreview(true)     // turn on; dispatches 'cursorpreviewchange' event\nwc.cursorPreview(false)    // turn off\n```\n\nThe lib doesn't render the cursor element itself (that's still wiring-side DOM concern), but it owns the on/off boolean and fires `'cursorpreviewchange'` on `targetEl` so the wiring can react. Default is `!_mobileDetected` \u2014 same auto-detection as mobile mode, so desktop users get the cursor by default and touch users don't.\n\nThe mobile-mode toggle now routes through `wc.cursorPreview(!on)` instead of touching the element directly. Both sides stay in sync regardless of which API entry point you flip.\n\n**Bonus:** the paper brush (`pigment === -5`, added in v0.32) now has its own cursor styling \u2014 hollow ring with paper-tinted fill \u2014 so you can tell at a glance you're about to clear pixels rather than paint.\n\n## Fade actually erases now \u2014 also a real bug\n\nPure multiplicative decay (`d[k][i] *= fadeFactor` where `fadeFactor < 1`) asymptotes to zero but **never reaches it**. The canvas was retaining a faint residual forever, and combined with the v0.33 fade-on-dry render forcing, the loop was forcing a render every fade tick on a \"dry\" canvas that wasn't actually getting any clearer.\n\nThe fix uses a constant that's been sitting unused since v0.18: `FADE_SKIP_BELOW = 0.001`. After each multiplicative decay step, hard-cut any channel below that threshold to exactly zero:\n\n```js\nlet v0 = d0[i] * f; d0[i] = v0 < floor ? 0 : v0;\n```\n\nWith the default 4-second half-life, a saturated cell (`d \u2248 0.5`) reaches the floor in ~9 half-lives (~36 seconds) and snaps to zero. The total fade time is now bounded \u2014 eventually `lastTotalSuspended \u2248 0`, the sim goes idle for real, and the fade-on-dry render stops firing (nothing left to render).\n\n**Verified in Node:** 200 manual fade ticks on a populated `d[]` array \u2192 total pigment is exactly 0. No residual.\n\n## Refactor noted\n\nThe wiring's cursor block had `_cursorVisible` doing double duty for \"currently has opacity > 0\" and \"should this exist at all\". Split into two flags:\n- `_cursorVisible` \u2014 current opacity state (set by pointer move/leave)\n- `_cursorEnabled` \u2014 allowed to render at all (set by `applyCursorPreviewEnabled`)\n\nThe conflation is part of why the `display: none` bug went unnoticed \u2014 both flags pointed the same direction in the common case, so the code \"worked\" when the toggle was off and \"didn't work\" when it was on, which read like a mobile-mode quirk rather than a CSS bug.",
    "citations": []
  }
]

-->\n<script>\n// === LIBRARY INLINE PLACEHOLDER ===\n// (Filled by the build script \u2014 see build process at bottom of file\n// for the standalone version. For this development version, the lib\n// is loaded as a separate script tag in the build step.)\n</script>\n\n<!-- \u2554\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2557 -->\n<!-- \u2551  Multi-Watercolor v0.1 \u2014 multi-instance wiring               \u2551 -->\n<!-- \u255a\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u255d -->\n<script>\n\"use strict\";\n\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------\n// SVG sources \u2014 three sketches, each thematically distinct so the\n// thumbnails read as separate \"projects\". Embedded inline so this is a\n// fully standalone file. Real-world usage would fetch these from a CDN\n// or a project-data manifest.\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------\nconst SVGS = {\n  // ORCHARD \u2014 a stylized tree: trunk + a few branches + leaf cluster.\n  // Uses multiple <path> elements so the per-stroke pause from\n  // perStrokePauseMs renders as visible pen-lift moments.\n  orchard:\n    '<svg viewBox=\"0 0 100 100\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\">' +\n      // Tree trunk\n      '<path d=\"M 50 85 L 50 50\"/>' +\n      // Two branches\n      '<path d=\"M 50 60 L 35 45\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 50 55 L 65 40\"/>' +\n      // Leaf cluster \u2014 a soft cloud shape\n      '<path d=\"M 30 35 C 25 25, 40 18, 50 25 C 60 18, 75 25, 70 35 C 75 45, 60 48, 50 40 C 40 48, 25 45, 30 35 Z\"/>' +\n      // Ground line\n      '<path d=\"M 10 85 L 90 85\"/>' +\n    '</svg>',\n\n  // HARBOR \u2014 water, a sailboat outline, a sun.\n  harbor:\n    '<svg viewBox=\"0 0 100 100\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\">' +\n      // Sun\n      '<path d=\"M 75 25 m -10 0 a 10 10 0 1 0 20 0 a 10 10 0 1 0 -20 0\"/>' +\n      // Sailboat hull\n      '<path d=\"M 25 65 Q 50 75 70 65 L 65 60 L 30 60 Z\"/>' +\n      // Mast + sail\n      '<path d=\"M 47 60 L 47 30\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 47 30 L 47 55 L 62 55 Z\"/>' +\n      // Water lines\n      '<path d=\"M 10 75 Q 20 73 30 75 T 50 75 T 70 75 T 90 75\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 10 82 Q 20 80 30 82 T 50 82 T 70 82 T 90 82\"/>' +\n    '</svg>',\n\n  // MEADOW \u2014 rolling hills, sun, a few flower marks.\n  meadow:\n    '<svg viewBox=\"0 0 100 100\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\">' +\n      // Sun\n      '<path d=\"M 25 25 m -8 0 a 8 8 0 1 0 16 0 a 8 8 0 1 0 -16 0\"/>' +\n      // Distant hill\n      '<path d=\"M 0 65 Q 30 55 55 60 Q 80 65 100 58\"/>' +\n      // Near hill\n      '<path d=\"M 0 80 Q 25 72 50 78 Q 75 84 100 74\"/>' +\n      // Three flower stems\n      '<path d=\"M 30 90 L 30 78\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 50 92 L 50 80\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 70 91 L 70 79\"/>' +\n      // Flower heads \u2014 small circles\n      '<path d=\"M 30 77 m -2 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 4 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 -4 0\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 50 79 m -2 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 4 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 -4 0\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 70 78 m -2 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 4 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 -4 0\"/>' +\n    '</svg>',\n};\n\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Boot \u2014 wait for the library to be available. Both inline scripts\n// execute in order, but in a real loader-vs-defer setup we'd want to\n// guard against the lib loading async.\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------\nfunction boot() {\n  if (typeof window.Watercolor === 'undefined') {\n    return setTimeout(boot, 30);\n  }\n\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // HERO \u2014 full-viewport canvas with a continuous animation.\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // canvasScale: 1 \u2192 match host pixels 1:1 \u2192 big simulation grid \u2192 CPU\n  // expensive. Knock it to ~0.6 so the hero stays smooth even on\n  // modest hardware. The CSS upscales it for free.\n  const hero = window.Watercolor.create(document.getElementById('hero'), {\n    canvasScale: 0.6,\n    pointer: false,         // hero is decorative; clicks pass through\n    cursorPreview: false,   // no brush cursor on the background\n  });\n  // partlyCloudy is the gentlest of the looped sky animations \u2014\n  // soft moving wash of color, doesn't compete with the thumbnails\n  // for attention. Other options worth trying: 'rainy', 'sunny',\n  // 'windy', or a 'visualization' like 'kaleidoscope'.\n  hero.setAnimation('partlyCloudy');\n\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // THUMBNAILS \u2014 three small canvases, each traced from an SVG.\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Each runs as its own Watercolor instance with sketchMode +\n  // a deliberate trace animation (penStroke easing, 1500ms total,\n  // 200ms pauses between paths so the trace reads as a hand-drawing\n  // motion). After the trace finishes, each instance idles until the\n  // user clicks; on click it obliterates and \"navigates\".\n  const thumbConfigs = [\n    { hostId: 'thumb-1', project: 'orchard', pigment: 'blue'   },\n    { hostId: 'thumb-2', project: 'harbor',  pigment: 'rose'   },\n    { hostId: 'thumb-3', project: 'meadow',  pigment: 'yellow' },\n  ];\n\n  const thumbInstances = thumbConfigs.map((cfg, i) => {\n    const hostEl = document.getElementById(cfg.hostId);\n    const wc = window.Watercolor.create(hostEl, {\n      // Smaller canvases \u2014 bump scale up for crisper line rendering.\n      canvasScale: 0.7,\n      pointer: false,           // thumbnail click handled by parent card\n      cursorPreview: false,     // no brush cursor over thumbnails\n    });\n    wc.sketchMode();            // felt-pen settings bundle from v0.30\n\n    // Stagger the trace start so the three thumbnails don't all paint\n    // simultaneously \u2014 visually staggered draws are more interesting\n    // than synchronized ones.\n    setTimeout(() => {\n      wc.traceSVG(SVGS[cfg.project], {\n        pigment: cfg.pigment,\n        durationMs: 1500,\n        easing: 'penStroke',\n        perStrokePauseMs: 200,\n      });\n    }, 250 + i * 350);\n\n    return { ...cfg, wc, hostEl };\n  });\n\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Click handlers \u2014 obliterate then \"navigate\"\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // The user said the future v2 is \"load paintings from a raster\n  // image\" \u2014 i.e. swap the hero canvas to show the project content.\n  // For v0.1 we just obliterate and log the project name. The card's\n  // outer <article> is the click target so the whole card is hot,\n  // not just the canvas.\n  thumbInstances.forEach((thumb) => {\n    const card = thumb.hostEl.closest('.thumb');\n    if (!card) return;\n    let busy = false;\n    card.addEventListener('click', async (e) => {\n      if (busy) return;\n      busy = true;\n\n      // Convert click point on the card to grid coords so the\n      // obliterate splash centers where the user clicked.\n      const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\n      const localX = e.clientX - rect.left;\n      const localY = e.clientY - rect.top;\n      const grid = thumb.wc.toGrid(localX, localY);\n\n      // Water-mode obliterate \u2014 pigment lifts and diffuses out,\n      // ~500ms. Pigment mode (Shift) and paper mode (Alt) are\n      // available too; this v0.1 demo uses just water for the\n      // signature transition feel.\n      await thumb.wc.obliterate({\n        mode: 'water',\n        durationMs: 500,\n        x: grid.gx, y: grid.gy,\n      });\n\n      console.log('Project clicked:', thumb.project,\n                  '\u2014 v2 would navigate here.');\n\n      // Re-draw the sketch after a brief beat so the demo remains\n      // interactive without a page reload. In a real portfolio,\n      // this is where you'd swap routes.\n      setTimeout(() => {\n        thumb.wc.reset();\n        thumb.wc.sketchMode();\n        thumb.wc.traceSVG(SVGS[thumb.project], {\n          pigment: thumb.pigment,\n          durationMs: 1200,\n          easing: 'penStroke',\n          perStrokePauseMs: 150,\n        });\n        busy = false;\n      }, 900);\n    });\n  });\n\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Window resize \u2014 instances rebuild their grids automatically on\n  // resize via the library's internal observer, so nothing to wire\n  // here. Each host element's size is taken from CSS layout.\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n}\n\nif (document.readyState === 'loading') {\n  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', boot);\n} else {\n  boot();\n}\n</script>\n\n</body>\n</html>\n"
    },

\\u255a\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u255d -->\\n<script>\\n// === LIBRARY INLINE PLACEHOLDER ===\\n// (Filled by the build script \\u2014 see build process at bottom of file\\n// for the standalone version. For this development version, the lib\\n// is loaded as a separate script tag in the build step.)\\n</script>\\n\\n<!-- \\u2554\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2557 -->\\n<!-- \\u2551  Multi-Watercolor v0.1 \\u2014 multi-instance wiring               \\u2551 -->\\n<!-- \\u255a\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u2550\\u255d -->\\n<script>\\n\\\"use strict\\\";\\n\\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// SVG sources \\u2014 three sketches, each thematically distinct so the\\n// thumbnails read as separate \\\"projects\\\". Embedded inline so this is a\\n// fully standalone file. Real-world usage would fetch these from a CDN\\n// or a project-data manifest.\\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------\\nconst SVGS = {\\n  // ORCHARD \\u2014 a stylized tree: trunk + a few branches + leaf cluster.\\n  // Uses multiple <path> elements so the per-stroke pause from\\n  // perStrokePauseMs renders as visible pen-lift moments.\\n  orchard:\\n    '<svg viewBox=\\\"0 0 100 100\\\" xmlns=\\\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\\\">' +\\n      // Tree trunk\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 50 85 L 50 50\\\"/>' +\\n      // Two branches\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 50 60 L 35 45\\\"/>' +\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 50 55 L 65 40\\\"/>' +\\n      // Leaf cluster \\u2014 a soft cloud shape\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 30 35 C 25 25, 40 18, 50 25 C 60 18, 75 25, 70 35 C 75 45, 60 48, 50 40 C 40 48, 25 45, 30 35 Z\\\"/>' +\\n      // Ground line\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 10 85 L 90 85\\\"/>' +\\n    '</svg>',\\n\\n  // HARBOR \\u2014 water, a sailboat outline, a sun.\\n  harbor:\\n    '<svg viewBox=\\\"0 0 100 100\\\" xmlns=\\\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\\\">' +\\n      // Sun\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 75 25 m -10 0 a 10 10 0 1 0 20 0 a 10 10 0 1 0 -20 0\\\"/>' +\\n      // Sailboat hull\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 25 65 Q 50 75 70 65 L 65 60 L 30 60 Z\\\"/>' +\\n      // Mast + sail\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 47 60 L 47 30\\\"/>' +\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 47 30 L 47 55 L 62 55 Z\\\"/>' +\\n      // Water lines\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 10 75 Q 20 73 30 75 T 50 75 T 70 75 T 90 75\\\"/>' +\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 10 82 Q 20 80 30 82 T 50 82 T 70 82 T 90 82\\\"/>' +\\n    '</svg>',\\n\\n  // MEADOW \\u2014 rolling hills, sun, a few flower marks.\\n  meadow:\\n    '<svg viewBox=\\\"0 0 100 100\\\" xmlns=\\\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\\\">' +\\n      // Sun\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 25 25 m -8 0 a 8 8 0 1 0 16 0 a 8 8 0 1 0 -16 0\\\"/>' +\\n      // Distant hill\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 0 65 Q 30 55 55 60 Q 80 65 100 58\\\"/>' +\\n      // Near hill\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 0 80 Q 25 72 50 78 Q 75 84 100 74\\\"/>' +\\n      // Three flower stems\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 30 90 L 30 78\\\"/>' +\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 50 92 L 50 80\\\"/>' +\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 70 91 L 70 79\\\"/>' +\\n      // Flower heads \\u2014 small circles\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 30 77 m -2 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 4 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 -4 0\\\"/>' +\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 50 79 m -2 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 4 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 -4 0\\\"/>' +\\n      '<path d=\\\"M 70 78 m -2 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 4 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 -4 0\\\"/>' +\\n    '</svg>',\\n};\\n\\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Boot \\u2014 wait for the library to be available. Both inline scripts\\n// execute in order, but in a real loader-vs-defer setup we'd want to\\n// guard against the lib loading async.\\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------\\nfunction boot() {\\n  if (typeof window.Watercolor === 'undefined') {\\n    return setTimeout(boot, 30);\\n  }\\n\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  // HERO \\u2014 full-viewport canvas with a continuous animation.\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  // canvasScale: 1 \\u2192 match host pixels 1:1 \\u2192 big simulation grid \\u2192 CPU\\n  // expensive. Knock it to ~0.6 so the hero stays smooth even on\\n  // modest hardware. The CSS upscales it for free.\\n  const hero = window.Watercolor.create(document.getElementById('hero'), {\\n    canvasScale: 0.6,\\n    pointer: false,         // hero is decorative; clicks pass through\\n    cursorPreview: false,   // no brush cursor on the background\\n  });\\n  // partlyCloudy is the gentlest of the looped sky animations \\u2014\\n  // soft moving wash of color, doesn't compete with the thumbnails\\n  // for attention. Other options worth trying: 'rainy', 'sunny',\\n  // 'windy', or a 'visualization' like 'kaleidoscope'.\\n  hero.setAnimation('partlyCloudy');\\n\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  // THUMBNAILS \\u2014 three small canvases, each traced from an SVG.\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  // Each runs as its own Watercolor instance with sketchMode +\\n  // a deliberate trace animation (penStroke easing, 1500ms total,\\n  // 200ms pauses between paths so the trace reads as a hand-drawing\\n  // motion). After the trace finishes, each instance idles until the\\n  // user clicks; on click it obliterates and \\\"navigates\\\".\\n  const thumbConfigs = [\\n    { hostId: 'thumb-1', project: 'orchard', pigment: 'blue'   },\\n    { hostId: 'thumb-2', project: 'harbor',  pigment: 'rose'   },\\n    { hostId: 'thumb-3', project: 'meadow',  pigment: 'yellow' },\\n  ];\\n\\n  const thumbInstances = thumbConfigs.map((cfg, i) => {\\n    const hostEl = document.getElementById(cfg.hostId);\\n    const wc = window.Watercolor.create(hostEl, {\\n      // Smaller canvases \\u2014 bump scale up for crisper line rendering.\\n      canvasScale: 0.7,\\n      pointer: false,           // thumbnail click handled by parent card\\n      cursorPreview: false,     // no brush cursor over thumbnails\\n    });\\n    wc.sketchMode();            // felt-pen settings bundle from v0.30\\n\\n    // Stagger the trace start so the three thumbnails don't all paint\\n    // simultaneously \\u2014 visually staggered draws are more interesting\\n    // than synchronized ones.\\n    setTimeout(() => {\\n      wc.traceSVG(SVGS[cfg.project], {\\n        pigment: cfg.pigment,\\n        durationMs: 1500,\\n        easing: 'penStroke',\\n        perStrokePauseMs: 200,\\n      });\\n    }, 250 + i * 350);\\n\\n    return { ...cfg, wc, hostEl };\\n  });\\n\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  // Click handlers \\u2014 obliterate then \\\"navigate\\\"\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  // The user said the future v2 is \\\"load paintings from a raster\\n  // image\\\" \\u2014 i.e. swap the hero canvas to show the project content.\\n  // For v0.1 we just obliterate and log the project name. The card's\\n  // outer <article> is the click target so the whole card is hot,\\n  // not just the canvas.\\n  thumbInstances.forEach((thumb) => {\\n    const card = thumb.hostEl.closest('.thumb');\\n    if (!card) return;\\n    let busy = false;\\n    card.addEventListener('click', async (e) => {\\n      if (busy) return;\\n      busy = true;\\n\\n      // Convert click point on the card to grid coords so the\\n      // obliterate splash centers where the user clicked.\\n      const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\\n      const localX = e.clientX - rect.left;\\n      const localY = e.clientY - rect.top;\\n      const grid = thumb.wc.toGrid(localX, localY);\\n\\n      // Water-mode obliterate \\u2014 pigment lifts and diffuses out,\\n      // ~500ms. Pigment mode (Shift) and paper mode (Alt) are\\n      // available too; this v0.1 demo uses just water for the\\n      // signature transition feel.\\n      await thumb.wc.obliterate({\\n        mode: 'water',\\n        durationMs: 500,\\n        x: grid.gx, y: grid.gy,\\n      });\\n\\n      console.log('Project clicked:', thumb.project,\\n                  '\\u2014 v2 would navigate here.');\\n\\n      // Re-draw the sketch after a brief beat so the demo remains\\n      // interactive without a page reload. In a real portfolio,\\n      // this is where you'd swap routes.\\n      setTimeout(() => {\\n        thumb.wc.reset();\\n        thumb.wc.sketchMode();\\n        thumb.wc.traceSVG(SVGS[thumb.project], {\\n          pigment: thumb.pigment,\\n          durationMs: 1200,\\n          easing: 'penStroke',\\n          perStrokePauseMs: 150,\\n        });\\n        busy = false;\\n      }, 900);\\n    });\\n  });\\n\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  // Window resize \\u2014 instances rebuild their grids automatically on\\n  // resize via the library's internal observer, so nothing to wire\\n  // here. Each host element's size is taken from CSS layout.\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n}\\n\\nif (document.readyState === 'loading') {\\n  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', boot);\\n} else {\\n  boot();\\n}\\n</script>\\n\\n</body>\\n</html>\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/multi-watercolor-v0.1.html\"}"

    "text": "**Both shipped.**\n\n## Cursor transparency\n\nThe rose / yellow / blue cursors were opaque hex fills, which meant while you were painting them you couldn't actually see what you were putting down \u2014 the cursor disc covered the freshly-deposited pigment. Now they match the alpha treatment the water and paper brushes already had:\n\n```js\nconst colors = [\n  'rgba(201, 94, 132, 0.35)',   // rose\n  'rgba(232, 196, 74, 0.35)',   // yellow\n  'rgba(80, 137, 176, 0.35)',   // blue / cerulean\n];\n```\n\n0.35 alpha \u2014 same hue, color hint stays legible, painting in progress stays visible.\n\n## Multi-Watercolor v0.1\n\nA clean standalone file at `multi-watercolor-v0.1.html`. Four independent Watercolor instances driving:\n\n1. **Hero canvas** \u2014 full viewport, `position: fixed; inset: 0`, runs `setAnimation('partlyCloudy')` continuously. Scale 0.6 (CPU economy on a big canvas; the CSS upscales pixels for free). `pointer: false` so clicks pass through to the cards on top. `cursorPreview: false` so the brush cursor doesn't follow the user around the background.\n\n2. **Three thumbnail canvases** \u2014 small (220px desktop / 100px mobile) cards positioned over the hero. Each gets its own `Watercolor.create()` instance with `sketchMode()` plus a deliberate `traceSVG` call:\n   ```js\n   wc.traceSVG(svgText, {\n     pigment: 'blue|rose|yellow',\n     durationMs: 1500,\n     easing: 'penStroke',\n     perStrokePauseMs: 200,\n   });\n   ```\n   The trace starts are staggered by `250 + i * 350` ms so the three thumbnails draw sequentially rather than simultaneously \u2014 visually more interesting than synchronized motion.\n\n**Click behavior on each card:**\n```js\nconst grid = thumb.wc.toGrid(localX, localY);\nawait thumb.wc.obliterate({ mode: 'water', durationMs: 500, x: grid.gx, y: grid.gy });\nconsole.log('Project clicked:', thumb.project);\n// ... 900ms later, redraw the sketch (so the demo stays interactive)\n```\n\nThe obliterate centers at the click position (`toGrid` converts client coords to grid coords). After the 500ms rinse, console.log fires \u2014 in your real portfolio this is where you'd `router.push(projectUrl)`. The `busy` flag prevents clicks during the in-flight transition.\n\n**Layout per your spec:**\n\n- **Desktop (\u2265 760px):** `.thumbnails { flex-direction: column; top: 50%; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%) }` \u2014 three cards stacked vertically (3 rows \u00d7 1 column), centered horizontally and vertically.\n- **Mobile (\u2264 760px):** `.thumbnails { flex-direction: row; bottom: 24px; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%) }` \u2014 three cards in a row (1 row \u00d7 3 columns), anchored near the bottom of the viewport so the hero animation owns the upper area.\n\n**SVGs are inline** \u2014 three thematic sketches (Orchard / Harbor / Meadow), each with multiple `<path>` elements so the `perStrokePauseMs: 200` actually does something visible (pen-lift moments between strokes). Real-world usage would fetch them from a CDN; for a self-contained demo file, inline is cleaner.\n\n**Verified in Node:** 4 instances spin up cleanly, state is isolated per-instance (changing brushSize on thumb 0 doesn't affect thumb 1 or hero), each thumb can trace + obliterate independently. The library's per-closure state isolation works as designed for the multi-instance use case.\n\n**Performance note:** the hero runs RAF continuously because the animation is active. The three thumbnails go idle after their initial trace completes (~1.5s) and consume effectively zero CPU until clicked \u2014 `shouldRunSim()` returns false once their cells settle, the per-frame `simStep` is skipped, and only the (now-bounded-by-fade-floor) fade runs at 10Hz if enabled.\n\nFor v2 (\"load paintings from a raster image\"): the obliterate call already returns a Promise, and the thumbnail instances are first-class \u2014 you can `instance.reset()` and then `instance.paintAt()` per sampled pixel from a raster source to replace the SVG-traced sketch with a raster-traced one. left: 50%;\n    bottom: 24px;\n    transform: translateX(-50%);\n    gap: 12px;\n  }\n  .thumb {\n    width: 100px; height: 100px;\n  }\n  .thumb-title {\n    font-size: 8.5px;\n    padding: 5px 8px 6px;\n    letter-spacing: 0.06em;\n  }\n  .thumb-title::before {\n    width: 4px; height: 4px;\n    margin-right: 5px;\n  }\n  .hint {\n    top: 16px; bottom: auto;\n    font-size: 9px;\n    padding: 6px 12px;\n  }\n  .help { padding: 16px 20px; font-size: 10px; }\n}\n</style>\n</head>\n<body>\n\n<!-- Hero \u2014 paintable background canvas. -->\n<div class=\"hero\" id=\"hero\" aria-label=\"Drawing surface\"></div>\n\n<!-- Project thumbnails \u2014 over the hero, clickable. -->\n<div class=\"thumbnails\" aria-label=\"Projects\">\n  <article class=\"thumb\" data-project=\"orchard\" data-title=\"Orchard\">\n    <div class=\"thumb-canvas\" id=\"thumb-1\"></div>\n    <div class=\"thumb-title\">Orchard</div>\n  </article>\n  <article class=\"thumb\" data-project=\"harbor\" data-title=\"Harbor\">\n    <div class=\"thumb-canvas\" id=\"thumb-2\"></div>\n    <div class=\"thumb-title\">Harbor</div>\n  </article>\n  <article class=\"thumb\" data-project=\"meadow\" data-title=\"Meadow\">\n    <div class=\"thumb-canvas\" id=\"thumb-3\"></div>\n    <div class=\"thumb-title\">Meadow</div>\n  </article>\n</div>\n\n<!-- Ephemeral hint. Auto-dismissed on first paint or 4s timeout. -->\n<div class=\"hint\" id=\"hint\">draw anywhere &nbsp;\u00b7&nbsp; press H for shortcuts</div>\n\n<!-- Help overlay \u2014 keyboard shortcuts. Hidden until H is pressed. -->\n<div class=\"help\" id=\"help\" role=\"dialog\" aria-hidden=\"true\" aria-label=\"Keyboard shortcuts\">\n  <h2>Keyboard shortcuts</h2>\n  <dl>\n    <dt>1</dt><dd>Rose brush</dd>\n    <dt>2</dt><dd>Yellow brush</dd>\n    <dt>3</dt><dd>Blue brush</dd>\n    <dt>4</dt><dd>Water brush (lifts pigment)</dd>\n    <dt>R</dt><dd>Reset the hero canvas</dd>\n    <dt>H</dt><dd>Show / hide this overlay</dd>\n  </dl>\n</div>\n\n<!-- \u2554\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2557 \u255a\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u255d -->\n<script>\n// === LIBRARY INLINE PLACEHOLDER ===\n</script>\n\n<!-- \u2554\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2557 -->\n<!-- \u2551  Multi-Watercolor v0.2 \u2014 multi-instance wiring + paintable   \u2551 -->\n<!-- \u255a\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u255d -->\n<script>\n\"use strict\";\n\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------\n// SVG sources \u2014 unchanged from v0.1.\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------\nconst SVGS = {\n  orchard:\n    '<svg viewBox=\"0 0 100 100\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\">' +\n      '<path d=\"M 50 85 L 50 50\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 50 60 L 35 45\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 50 55 L 65 40\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 30 35 C 25 25, 40 18, 50 25 C 60 18, 75 25, 70 35 C 75 45, 60 48, 50 40 C 40 48, 25 45, 30 35 Z\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 10 85 L 90 85\"/>' +\n    '</svg>',\n  harbor:\n    '<svg viewBox=\"0 0 100 100\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\">' +\n      '<path d=\"M 75 25 m -10 0 a 10 10 0 1 0 20 0 a 10 10 0 1 0 -20 0\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 25 65 Q 50 75 70 65 L 65 60 L 30 60 Z\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 47 60 L 47 30\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 47 30 L 47 55 L 62 55 Z\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 10 75 Q 20 73 30 75 T 50 75 T 70 75 T 90 75\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 10 82 Q 20 80 30 82 T 50 82 T 70 82 T 90 82\"/>' +\n    '</svg>',\n  meadow:\n    '<svg viewBox=\"0 0 100 100\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\">' +\n      '<path d=\"M 25 25 m -8 0 a 8 8 0 1 0 16 0 a 8 8 0 1 0 -16 0\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 0 65 Q 30 55 55 60 Q 80 65 100 58\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 0 80 Q 25 72 50 78 Q 75 84 100 74\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 30 90 L 30 78\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 50 92 L 50 80\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 70 91 L 70 79\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 30 77 m -2 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 4 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 -4 0\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 50 79 m -2 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 4 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 -4 0\"/>' +\n      '<path d=\"M 70 78 m -2 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 4 0 a 2 2 0 1 0 -4 0\"/>' +\n    '</svg>',\n};\n\nfunction boot() {\n  if (typeof window.Watercolor === 'undefined') {\n    return setTimeout(boot, 30);\n  }\n\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // HERO \u2014 full-viewport, NOW PAINTABLE.\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // The key difference from v0.1: `pointer: true` enables the lib's\n  // pointer handlers on the hero canvas, so mouse / touch / pen drag\n  // events become paint strokes. The thumbnail cards still sit above\n  // in z-order, so DOM event routing handles the \"click on a card =\n  // navigate, click on background = paint\" decision for free.\n  //\n  // No animation is set this time \u2014 the canvas starts blank. The user\n  // is the animation. (If you want a soft running background instead,\n  // call hero.setAnimation('partlyCloudy') here; the painting and the\n  // animation will compose.)\n  const hero = window.Watercolor.create(document.getElementById('hero'), {\n    canvasScale: 0.6,\n    pointer: true,            // \u2190 v0.2: drawable\n    cursorPreview: false,     // hero uses native crosshair (set in CSS)\n                              // \u2014 no DOM cursor preview, keeping the\n                              //   demo's no-UI promise.\n  });\n  // Tweak default brush state for the multi-canvas context. The lib's\n  // defaults are tuned for a focused single-canvas painting app; on\n  // a portfolio-style background, a slightly bigger, slightly drier\n  // brush feels nicer for casual finger painting.\n  hero.brushSize(60);\n  hero.paintLoad(0.85);\n  hero.waterLoad(1.1);\n  hero.pigment('rose');\n\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // THUMBNAILS \u2014 unchanged from v0.1.\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  const thumbConfigs = [\n    { hostId: 'thumb-1', project: 'orchard', pigment: 'blue'   },\n    { hostId: 'thumb-2', project: 'harbor',  pigment: 'rose'   },\n    { hostId: 'thumb-3', project: 'meadow',  pigment: 'yellow' },\n  ];\n  const thumbInstances = thumbConfigs.map((cfg, i) => {\n    const hostEl = document.getElementById(cfg.hostId);\n    const wc = window.Watercolor.create(hostEl, {\n      canvasScale: 0.7,\n      pointer: false,\n      cursorPreview: false,\n    });\n    wc.sketchMode();\n    setTimeout(() => {\n      wc.traceSVG(SVGS[cfg.project], {\n        pigment: cfg.pigment,\n        durationMs: 1500,\n        easing: 'penStroke',\n        perStrokePauseMs: 200,\n      });\n    }, 250 + i * 350);\n    return { ...cfg, wc, hostEl };\n  });\n\n  thumbInstances.forEach((thumb) => {\n    const card = thumb.hostEl.closest('.thumb');\n    if (!card) return;\n    let busy = false;\n    card.addEventListener('click', async (e) => {\n      if (busy) return;\n      busy = true;\n      const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\n      const grid = thumb.wc.toGrid(e.clientX - rect.left, e.clientY - rect.top);\n      await thumb.wc.obliterate({\n        mode: 'water', durationMs: 500,\n        x: grid.gx, y: grid.gy,\n      });\n      console.log('Project clicked:', thumb.project,\n                  '\u2014 v2 would navigate here.');\n      setTimeout(() => {\n        thumb.wc.reset();\n        thumb.wc.sketchMode();\n        thumb.wc.traceSVG(SVGS[thumb.project], {\n          pigment: thumb.pigment,\n          durationMs: 1200,\n          easing: 'penStroke',\n          perStrokePauseMs: 150,\n        });\n        busy = false;\n      }, 900);\n    });\n  });\n\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // FIRST-PAINT HINT \u2014 fades on first paint OR after 4 seconds.\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  const hint = document.getElementById('hint');\n  let hintGone = false;\n  function dismissHint() {\n    if (hintGone || !hint) return;\n    hintGone = true;\n    hint.classList.add('gone');\n    // Remove from layout after the fade completes so it doesn't catch\n    // accidental focus / screen-reader noise.\n    setTimeout(() => { if (hint.parentNode) hint.parentNode.removeChild(hint); }, 800);\n  }\n  // Fade after 4 seconds even if the user never paints.\n  setTimeout(dismissHint, 4000);\n  // Or fade the instant any paint happens on the hero. The hero\n  // canvas's pointerdown is the cleanest signal \u2014 we don't care\n  // whether the user actually drew anything, just whether they\n  // started interacting.\n  const heroEl = document.getElementById('hero');\n  heroEl.addEventListener('pointerdown', dismissHint, { once: true });\n\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS \u2014 the only \"UI\" in v0.2.\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Numbers 1\u20134 switch the hero brush; R resets; H toggles the help.\n  // Match the main app's convention: H keypress in non-input context.\n  const helpEl = document.getElementById('help');\n  function toggleHelp() {\n    if (!helpEl) return;\n    const open = helpEl.classList.toggle('open');\n    helpEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', String(!open));\n  }\n  document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n    // Ignore key events targeting form inputs (defensive \u2014 there are\n    // none in v0.2, but future additions might add them).\n    if (e.target && (e.target.tagName === 'INPUT' || e.target.tagName === 'TEXTAREA')) return;\n    const k = e.key;\n    if (k === '1') { hero.pigment('rose');   dismissHint(); }\n    else if (k === '2') { hero.pigment('yellow'); dismissHint(); }\n    else if (k === '3') { hero.pigment('blue');   dismissHint(); }\n    else if (k === '4') { hero.pigment('water');  dismissHint(); }\n    else if (k === 'r' || k === 'R') { hero.reset(); dismissHint(); }\n    else if (k === 'h' || k === 'H') { toggleHelp(); }\n    else if (k === 'Escape') {\n      if (helpEl.classList.contains('open')) toggleHelp();\n    }\n  });\n  // Clicking outside the help overlay closes it.\n  document.addEventListener('pointerdown', (e) => {\n    if (!helpEl.classList.contains('open')) return;\n    if (helpEl.contains(e.target)) return;\n    toggleHelp();\n  });\n}\n\nif (document.readyState === 'loading') {\n  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', boot);\n} else {\n  boot();\n}\n</script>\n\n</body>\n</html>\n",

on the hero canvas, so mouse / touch / pen drag\\n  // events become paint strokes. The thumbnail cards still sit above\\n  // in z-order, so DOM event routing handles the \\\"click on a card =\\n  // navigate, click on background = paint\\\" decision for free.\\n  //\\n  // No animation is set this time \\u2014 the canvas starts blank. The user\\n  // is the animation. (If you want a soft running background instead,\\n  // call hero.setAnimation('partlyCloudy') here; the painting and the\\n  // animation will compose.)\\n  const hero = window.Watercolor.create(document.getElementById('hero'), {\\n    canvasScale: 0.6,\\n    pointer: true,            // \\u2190 v0.2: drawable\\n    cursorPreview: false,     // hero uses native crosshair (set in CSS)\\n                              // \\u2014 no DOM cursor preview, keeping the\\n                              //   demo's no-UI promise.\\n  });\\n  // Tweak default brush state for the multi-canvas context. The lib's\\n  // defaults are tuned for a focused single-canvas painting app; on\\n  // a portfolio-style background, a slightly bigger, slightly drier\\n  // brush feels nicer for casual finger painting.\\n  hero.brushSize(60);\\n  hero.paintLoad(0.85);\\n  hero.waterLoad(1.1);\\n  hero.pigment('rose');\\n\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  // THUMBNAILS \\u2014 unchanged from v0.1.\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  const thumbConfigs = [\\n    { hostId: 'thumb-1', project: 'orchard', pigment: 'blue'   },\\n    { hostId: 'thumb-2', project: 'harbor',  pigment: 'rose'   },\\n    { hostId: 'thumb-3', project: 'meadow',  pigment: 'yellow' },\\n  ];\\n  const thumbInstances = thumbConfigs.map((cfg, i) => {\\n    const hostEl = document.getElementById(cfg.hostId);\\n    const wc = window.Watercolor.create(hostEl, {\\n      canvasScale: 0.7,\\n      pointer: false,\\n      cursorPreview: false,\\n    });\\n    wc.sketchMode();\\n    setTimeout(() => {\\n      wc.traceSVG(SVGS[cfg.project], {\\n        pigment: cfg.pigment,\\n        durationMs: 1500,\\n        easing: 'penStroke',\\n        perStrokePauseMs: 200,\\n      });\\n    }, 250 + i * 350);\\n    return { ...cfg, wc, hostEl };\\n  });\\n\\n  thumbInstances.forEach((thumb) => {\\n    const card = thumb.hostEl.closest('.thumb');\\n    if (!card) return;\\n    let busy = false;\\n    card.addEventListener('click', async (e) => {\\n      if (busy) return;\\n      busy = true;\\n      const rect = card.getBoundingClientRect();\\n      const grid = thumb.wc.toGrid(e.clientX - rect.left, e.clientY - rect.top);\\n      await thumb.wc.obliterate({\\n        mode: 'water', durationMs: 500,\\n        x: grid.gx, y: grid.gy,\\n      });\\n      console.log('Project clicked:', thumb.project,\\n                  '\\u2014 v2 would navigate here.');\\n      setTimeout(() => {\\n        thumb.wc.reset();\\n        thumb.wc.sketchMode();\\n        thumb.wc.traceSVG(SVGS[thumb.project], {\\n          pigment: thumb.pigment,\\n          durationMs: 1200,\\n          easing: 'penStroke',\\n          perStrokePauseMs: 150,\\n        });\\n        busy = false;\\n      }, 900);\\n    });\\n  });\\n\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  // FIRST-PAINT HINT \\u2014 fades on first paint OR after 4 seconds.\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  const hint = document.getElementById('hint');\\n  let hintGone = false;\\n  function dismissHint() {\\n    if (hintGone || !hint) return;\\n    hintGone = true;\\n    hint.classList.add('gone');\\n    // Remove from layout after the fade completes so it doesn't catch\\n    // accidental focus / screen-reader noise.\\n    setTimeout(() => { if (hint.parentNode) hint.parentNode.removeChild(hint); }, 800);\\n  }\\n  // Fade after 4 seconds even if the user never paints.\\n  setTimeout(dismissHint, 4000);\\n  // Or fade the instant any paint happens on the hero. The hero\\n  // canvas's pointerdown is the cleanest signal \\u2014 we don't care\\n  // whether the user actually drew anything, just whether they\\n  // started interacting.\\n  const heroEl = document.getElementById('hero');\\n  heroEl.addEventListener('pointerdown', dismissHint, { once: true });\\n\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  // KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS \\u2014 the only \\\"UI\\\" in v0.2.\\n  // ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n  // Numbers 1\\u20134 switch the hero brush; R resets; H toggles the help.\\n  // Match the main app's convention: H keypress in non-input context.\\n  const helpEl = document.getElementById('help');\\n  function toggleHelp() {\\n    if (!helpEl) return;\\n    const open = helpEl.classList.toggle('open');\\n    helpEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', String(!open));\\n  }\\n  document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\\n    // Ignore key events targeting form inputs (defensive \\u2014 there are\\n    // none in v0.2, but future additions might add them).\\n    if (e.target && (e.target.tagName === 'INPUT' || e.target.tagName === 'TEXTAREA')) return;\\n    const k = e.key;\\n    if (k === '1') { hero.pigment('rose');   dismissHint(); }\\n    else if (k === '2') { hero.pigment('yellow'); dismissHint(); }\\n    else if (k === '3') { hero.pigment('blue');   dismissHint(); }\\n    else if (k === '4') { hero.pigment('water');  dismissHint(); }\\n    else if (k === 'r' || k === 'R') { hero.reset(); dismissHint(); }\\n    else if (k === 'h' || k === 'H') { toggleHelp(); }\\n    else if (k === 'Escape') {\\n      if (helpEl.classList.contains('open')) toggleHelp();\\n    }\\n  });\\n  // Clicking outside the help overlay closes it.\\n  document.addEventListener('pointerdown', (e) => {\\n    if (!helpEl.classList.contains('open')) return;\\n    if (helpEl.contains(e.target)) return;\\n    toggleHelp();\\n  });\\n}\\n\\nif (document.readyState === 'loading') {\\n  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', boot);\\n} else {\\n  boot();\\n}\\n</script>\\n\\n</body>\\n</html>\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/multi-watercolor-v0.2.html\"}"

    "type": "text",
    "text": "**Multi-Watercolor v0.2 shipped.** Hero is now paintable while staying visually unchromed.\n\n## What changed from v0.1\n\n**One library option flip on the hero:**\n\n```js\nconst hero = window.Watercolor.create(document.getElementById('hero'), {\n  canvasScale: 0.6,\n  pointer: true,            // \u2190 v0.2: was false in v0.1\n  cursorPreview: false,\n});\n```\n\n`pointer: true` activates the library's built-in pointermove/pointerdown/pointerup handlers. The card thumbnails sit above the hero in z-order (`.thumb { z-index: 5 }` vs `.hero { z-index: 1 }`), so DOM event routing handles the disambiguation for free \u2014 pointer events over a card target the card and trigger its click handler; events over empty hero area target the canvas and the lib paints. No event-coordination code needed.\n\n**Plus a few hero brush defaults** that feel right for a background-canvas painting context (slightly larger brush, slightly drier paint, slightly more water for nice diffusion):\n\n```js\nhero.brushSize(60);\nhero.paintLoad(0.85);\nhero.waterLoad(1.1);\nhero.pigment('rose');\n```\n\nThe lib's defaults are tuned for a focused painting app. On a portfolio background where users will doodle casually with a finger or mouse, a bigger, looser brush feels more inviting.\n\n## The no-UI promise\n\nI removed the demo header and footer from v0.1 entirely. The page now contains:\n\n1. The hero canvas\n2. The three thumbnail cards\n3. A small ephemeral hint that says **\"draw anywhere \u00b7 press H for shortcuts\"**\n\nThe hint auto-dismisses on the first `pointerdown` on the hero OR after 4 seconds \u2014 whichever comes first. After that, the page is fully clean. Just the painted surface and the cards.\n\nFor interaction without buttons:\n\n```\n1 / 2 / 3   \u2192 rose / yellow / blue brush\n4           \u2192 water brush (lifts pigment)\nR           \u2192 reset hero canvas\nH           \u2192 toggle help overlay\nEsc         \u2192 close help\n```\n\nThe help overlay (`H`) is the only \"UI\" and it's keyboard-summoned only. By default it's `pointer-events: none`, `opacity: 0` \u2014 not just hidden, completely inert. Clicking outside it (when open) also closes it.\n\n## Why CSS `touch-action: none` matters here\n\nAdded to `html, body`:\n\n```css\ntouch-action: none;\n-webkit-user-select: none;\nuser-select: none;\n```\n\nOn a touch device, the browser by default consumes drag gestures for scrolling and pinch-zooming, never delivering them to JS. With `touch-action: none`, all touch events route to the canvas's pointer handlers \u2014 which is what you want for a drawing surface. Without it, finger-painting on mobile silently scrolls the page instead of leaving paint marks.\n\n`overflow: hidden` + `overscroll-behavior: none` round out the \"this is a drawing app, not a document\" feel.\n\n## Cursor affordance\n\nThe hero uses the native `cursor: crosshair` from CSS \u2014 no DOM cursor preview element to maintain. Decision: a DOM cursor preview would have been more thematic but would have required either (a) including the cursor-preview wiring code from the main app (a fair chunk of code for what's meant to be a minimal demo) or (b) building a stripped-down version. Native crosshair is the right call for v0.2 \u2014 it tells the user \"this is a drawing surface\" with one line of CSS. Future v0.3 could add the themed cursor if you want.\n\n**Verified in Node:** hero accepts custom brush state after create, all four pigment shortcuts route correctly through `pigment('rose'|'yellow'|'blue'|'water')`, reset clears state, instance isolation from v0.1 still holds (thumb instances unaffected by hero changes).\n\nThe only thing I couldn't verify in headless Node is the actual pointer-painting (no real pointer events in the stub) \u2014 but the library has been painting via the same `pointer: true` code path since v0.1 of the lib, so that's a known-good code path.",
